concerns.text: T030 mandates re-running entropy computation for r={0.15, 0.20, 0.25}*SD but fails to explicitly mandate re-computation of the Multiscale (AUC) aspect (FR-009). Without this, the task is ambiguous: an implementer might compute single-scale entropy, violating the spec's core metric definition. The task must explicitly state: 're-compute Multiscale Sample Entropy (AUC across scales) for each r value'.

T007 implements motion scrubbing and logging but omits the critical logic to exclude subjects with insufficient frames (<100 frames) as defined in spec.md Edge Cases. The task description only mentions filtering based on FD. An implementer following T007 strictly would process subjects with <100 frames, violating the spec's exclusion criteria. The task must include: 'exclude subjects with <100 remaining frames and log to missing_data.log'.

T015 references FR-001 for NaN handling but FR-001 only requires computing MSE. The specific edge case logic to 'flag the subject for manual review if >10% of parcels are invalid' (spec.md Edge Cases) is not assigned to any task. This creates a gap where the implementer has no instruction to implement the flagging logic, risking silent data degradation.

T030 (Sensitivity Analysis) requires re-running the full pipeline for three parameter sets but lacks a specific task to instrument and log peak RAM usage and runtime for these runs. SC-003 and SC-005 require reporting these metrics to verify compute feasibility. Without a dedicated task to capture these metrics during the sweep, the deliverable cannot be verified against the success criteria.

T001 'Create project structure' is too coarse. It covers the entire initialization phase. While the atomizer will split this, the task should be more specific to be immediately executable, e.g., 'Create directories: code/, data/raw, data/processed, data/logs, tests/, docs/'.
